Protein names: Indolethylamine N-methyltransferase (Indolamine N-methyltransferase) (EC 2.1.1.49) (EC 2.1.1.96) (Aromatic alkylamine N-methyltransferase) (Amine N-methyltransferase) (Arylamine N-methyltransferase) (Thioether S-methyltransferase) (TEMT)
Protein families: Class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily, NNMT/PNMT/TEMT family
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily. NNMT/PNMT/TEMT family. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=a tertiary amine + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = a methylated tertiary amine + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:53928, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:137982, ChEBI:CHEBI:137983; EC=2.1.1.49; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:10552930}; CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=a secondary amine + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = a methylated secondary amine + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:53924, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:137419, ChEBI:CHEBI:137984; EC=2.1.1.49; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:10552930}; CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=a primary amine + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = a methylated primary amine + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:23136, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:65296, ChEBI:CHEBI:131823; EC=2.1.1.49; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:10552930}; CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=dimethyl sulfide + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = trimethylsulfonium + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:19613, ChEBI:CHEBI:17434, ChEBI:CHEBI:17437, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789; EC=2.1.1.96; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:10552930};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Functions as a thioether S-methyltransferase and is active with a variety of thioethers and the corresponding selenium and tellurium compounds, including 3-methylthiopropionaldehyde, dimethyl selenide, dimethyl telluride, 2-methylthioethylamine, 2-methylthioethanol, methyl-n-propyl sulfide and diethyl sulfide. Plays an important role in the detoxification of selenium compounds (By similarity). Catalyzes the N-methylation of tryptamine and structurally related compounds. {ECO:0000250, ECO:0000269|PubMed:10552930}.
